item.title: Publications
item.description:
Selected publications. DBLP · Google Scholar · ORCID

2026

  • Boronat, A. (2026). Composing Clinical Activity Guidance for Multimorbidity via Bounded Relational Analysis. Proceedings of the 29th International Conference on Fundamental Approaches to Software Engineering (FASE 2026), LNCS. Springer. To appear.

  • Boronat, A. (2026). Bounded Structural Model Finding with Symbolic Data Constraints. Proceedings of the 15th International Workshop on Rewriting Logic and its Applications (WRLA 2026), LNCS. Springer. To appear.

  • Zschaler, S., Barnett, W., Boronat, A., García-Domínguez, A., Kolovos, D.S. (2026). The MDENet education platform: zero-install directed activities for learning MDE. Software and Systems Modeling, 25(1), pp. 287–313. [DOI]

2025

  • Boronat, A., Mustafa, J. (2025). MDRE-LLM: A Tool for Analyzing and Applying LLMs in Software Reverse Engineering. Proceedings of the 32nd IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering (SANER 2025), pp. 850–854. [DOI]

  • Boronat, A. (Editor), Fraser, G. (Editor) (2025). Fundamental Approaches to Software Engineering — 28th International Conference, FASE 2025. LNCS 15693. Springer. [DOI]

2024

  • Zschaler, S., Barnett, W., Boronat, A., García-Domínguez, A., Kolovos, D.S. (2024). Move your MDE teaching online: The MDENet Education Platform. Proceedings of MoDELS 2024 (Companion), pp. 6–10. [DOI]

  • Boronat, A. (2024). Exploring flexible models in agile MDE. Post Proceedings of the STAF 2023 Workshops, CEUR-WS, Vol. 3727. [PDF]

  • Boronat, A. (Editor), García-Domínguez, A., Hinkel, G., Lano, K., De Sanctis, M., Ferry, N., Mosser, S., Wimmer, M., Alfraihi, H., Kolahdouz Rahimi, S., Troya, J. (2024). Post Proceedings of the STAF 2023 Workshops (TTC 2023, MeSS 2023 and AgileMDE 2023). CEUR Workshop Proceedings, Vol. 3620. [URL]

2023

  • Boronat, A. (2023). EMF-Syncer: scalable maintenance of view models over heterogeneous data-centric software systems at run time. Software and Systems Modeling, 22(6), pp. 1949–1968. [DOI]

  • Boronat, A., Adeyemo, A., Mehtarizadeh, M., Zschaler, S. (2023). Automatic Inference of Smart Data Discovery Interfaces for Rare Disease Datasets. HEDA@STAF 2023, CEUR-WS. [PDF]

  • Barnett, W., Zschaler, S., Boronat, A., García-Domínguez, A., Kolovos, D.S. (2023). An Online Education Platform for Teaching MDE. Proceedings of MoDELS 2023 (Companion), pp. 114–121. [DOI]

  • Boronat, A. (2023). Asymmetric and Directed Bidirectional Transformation for Container Orchestrations with YAMTL and EMF-Syncer. Agile MDE/MeSS/TTC@STAF 2023, CEUR-WS, Vol. 3620. [PDF]

2022

  • Hinkel, G., García-Domínguez, A., Schöne, R., Boronat, A., Tisi, M., Le Calvar, T., Jouault, F., Marton, J., Nyíri, T., Antal, J.B., Elekes, M., Szárnyas, G. (2022). A Cross-Technology Benchmark for Incremental Graph Queries. Software and Systems Modeling, 21(2), pp. 755–804. [DOI] [PDF]

  • Boronat, A. (2022). Safe reuse in modelling language engineering using model subtyping with OCL constraints. Software and Systems Modeling, 22(3), pp. 797–818. [DOI]

  • Boronat, A. (Editor), García-Domínguez, A., Hinkel, G. (2022). Joint Proceedings of the 13th and 14th Tool Transformation Contests. CEUR Workshop Proceedings, Vol. 3089. [URL]

2021

  • Boronat, A. (2021). Incremental execution of rule-based model transformation. International Journal on Software Tools for Technology Transfer, 23(3). [DOI] [PDF]

  • Boronat, A. (2021). Incremental execution of relational transformation specifications in YAMTL: a case with laboratory workflows. Joint Proceedings of the 13th and 14th Tool Transformation Contests, CEUR-WS, Vol. 3089. [URL] [PDF]

2020

  • Boronat, A. (2020). EMF-Syncer solution to TTC 2020 round-trip migration case. Joint Proceedings of the 13th and 14th Tool Transformation Contests, CEUR-WS, Vol. 3089. [URL] [PDF]

2019

  • Boronat, A. (2019). Code-First Model-Driven Engineering: On the Agile Adoption of MDE Tooling. Proceedings of the 34th IEEE/ACM International Conference on Automated Software Engineering (ASE 2019), San Diego, CA. [DOI] [PDF] [Slides]

  • Boronat, A. (2019). Offline Delta-Driven Model Transformation with Dependency Injection. FASE 2019, LNCS 11424, pp. 134–150. [DOI] [PDF] [Slides]

  • Boronat, A. (2019). YAMTL Solution to the TTC 2019 BibTexToDocbook Case. Proceedings of the 12th Transformation Tool Contest (STAF 2019), CEUR-WS. [PDF]

  • Boronat, A. (2019). YAMTL Solution to the TTC 2019 TT2BDD Case. Proceedings of the 12th Transformation Tool Contest (STAF 2019), CEUR-WS. [PDF]

2018

  • Boronat, A. (2018). Expressive and Efficient Model Transformation with an Internal DSL of Xtend. Proceedings of the 21st ACM/IEEE International Conference on MoDELS, pp. 78–88. ACM. [DOI] [PDF] [Slides]

  • Boronat, A. (2018). A Formal Framework for Prototyping Executable Semantics in ATL. ICMT 2018, LNCS, pp. 157–172. [DOI] [PDF] [Slides]

  • Boronat, A. (2018). YAMTL Solution to the TTC 2018 Social Media Case. Proceedings of the 11th Transformation Tool Contest (TTC@STAF 2018), CEUR-WS, Vol. 2310, pp. 65–78. [PDF]

2017

  • Boronat, A. (2017). Structural model subtyping with OCL constraints. Proceedings of the 10th ACM SIGPLAN International Conference on Software Language Engineering (SLE 2017), Vancouver, BC, pp. 194–205. ACM. [DOI] [PDF] [Slides]

  • Boronat, A. (2017). Experimentation with a Big-Step Semantics for ATL Model Transformations. ICMT 2017, LNCS 10374, pp. 3–18. [DOI] [PDF] [Slides]

  • Boronat, A. (2017). Well-Behaved Model Transformations with Model Subtyping. Technical Report, CoRR abs/1703.08113. [URL]

2014

  • de Lara, J., Guerra, E., Boronat, A., Heckel, R., Torrini, P. (2014). Domain-specific discrete event modelling and simulation using graph transformation. Software and Systems Modeling, 13(1). [DOI]

  • El-Saber, N.A.S., Boronat, A. (2014). BPMN Formalization and Verification using Maude. BM-FA 2014. [DOI]

2013

  • Orejas, F., Boronat, A., Ehrig, H., Hermann, F., Schölzel, H. (2013). On Propagation-Based Concurrent Model Synchronization. Electronic Communications of the EASST, Vol. 57. [DOI]

  • Orejas, F., Boronat, A., Golas, U., Mylonakis, N. (2013). Checking Bisimilarity for Attributed Graph Transformation. FoSSaCS 2013, LNCS 7794. [DOI]

2012

2011

  • Bergmann, G., Boronat, A., Heckel, R., Torrini, P., Ráth, I., Varró, D. (2011). Advances in Model Transformations by Graph Transformation: Specification, Execution and Analysis. In: Rigorous Software Engineering for Service-Oriented Systems (SENSORIA), LNCS 6582, pp. 561–584. Springer. [DOI]

2010

  • Boronat, A., Meseguer, J. (2010). An Algebraic Semantics for MOF. Formal Aspects of Computing, 22(3), pp. 269–296. Springer. [DOI]

  • Boronat, A., Ölveczky, P.C. (2010). Formal Real-Time Model Transformations in MOMENT2. FASE 2010, LNCS 6013, pp. 29–43. [DOI]

  • Boronat, A., Bruni, R., Lluch-Lafuente, A., Montanari, U., Paolillo, G. (2010). Exploiting the Hierarchical Structure of Rule-Based Specifications for Decision Planning. FMOODS/FORTE 2010, LNCS 6117. [DOI]

  • Ölveczky, P.C., Boronat, A., Meseguer, J. (2010). Formal Semantics and Analysis of Behavioral AADL Models in Real-Time Maude. FMOODS/FORTE 2010, LNCS 6117, pp. 47–62. [DOI]

  • de Lara, J., Guerra, E., Boronat, A., Heckel, R., Torrini, P. (2010). Graph Transformation for Domain-Specific Discrete Event Time Simulation. ICGT 2010, LNCS 6372, pp. 266–281. [DOI]

2009

  • Boronat, A., Heckel, R., Meseguer, J. (2009). Rewriting Logic Semantics and Verification of Model Transformations. FASE 2009, LNCS 5503, pp. 18–33. [DOI]

  • Boronat, A., Meseguer, J. (2009). Algebraic Semantics of OCL-constrained Metamodel Specifications. TOOLS (47), LNBIP 33, pp. 96–115. [DOI]

  • Boronat, A., Meseguer, J. (2009). MOMENT2: EMF Model Transformations in Maude. JISBD 2009, pp. 178–179.

2008

2007

  • Boronat, A. (2007). MOMENT: a formal framework for MOdel manageMENT. PhD thesis, Universitat Politècnica de València (UPV), Spain.

2006

  • Boronat, A., Ramos, I., Carsí, J.A. (2004). Automatic Model Generation in Model Management. CIT 2004, LNCS 3308. [DOI]

item.title: Research
item.description:
Research Themes

YAMTL — A model-to-model transformation engine whose transformations are defined with an internal DSL of Xtend. Main strengths: interoperability with Java, expressiveness, and performance. Supports incremental execution. yamtl.github.io

EMF-Syncer — Facilitates the adoption of Model-Driven Engineering techniques and EMF-supported tooling in JVM programs. arturboronat.github.io/emf-syncer

MDE-subtyping — A tool that infers whether two metamodel specifications (EMF metamodel + OCL constraints) are compatible, enabling safe reuse of model management operations. mde-subtyping.github.io/web

FMA-ATL — An executable formalization of a large excerpt of the model transformation language ATL in Maude, using EMF and ATL as front-end languages. fma-atl.github.io

Funded opportunities — For those looking for research-oriented opportunities in model-driven engineering, AI/ML, and healthcare. arturboronat.github.io/funded-opportunities

Grants and Projects

Personalised Space Technology Exercise Platform (P-STEP) Funder: European Space Agency (ESA) · Role: Informatics lead · 2021–2023 · Award: £2M (whole project)

P-STEP is an eHealth service platform to deliver and monitor personalised exercise advice to patients living with long term health conditions. The platform provides warnings and guidance based on environmental conditions and location (from GNSS and EO sources) to modify recommended exercises and reduce risk. Through AI and optimally designed user interfaces, it promotes adherence to advice. The platform targets long-term respiratory and cardiac conditions, identified as clinical priorities in the NHS 10-year plan. Project page

P-STEP CCN1: iOS & Wearables Extension Funder: European Space Agency (ESA) · Role: Informatics lead (WP3000) · Nov 2024–Oct 2025 · Award: EUR 467,528 (ESA contribution; total project cost EUR 935,054)

Contract Change Notice 1 extends the P-STEP platform to iOS via Kotlin Multiplatform (KMP) and adds wearables integration (Terra API) for Apple Watch, Fitbit, Garmin, and Google Fit. The extension addresses a commercialisation barrier identified during pilot activities: 39% of prospective participants were excluded due to iOS-only access. Backend hardening, security, and data aggregation updates are also in scope. Project page

An AI-powered DSML for Data Discovery for Rare Diseases Funder: EPSRC network MDE-Net · Role: PI · Jun–Dec 2022 · Award: £4,950

Design of a domain-specific modelling language (DSML) and development of a proof-of-concept no-code development platform for rare disease medical researchers. Applies MDE productivity tools and AI-enabled optimization techniques. Project page

MDENet: The expert network for Model-Driven Engineering Funder: Engineering and Physical Sciences Research Council (EPSRC) · Role: Founding member · 2022–2023

The EPSRC network MDENet brings together research and practice in Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) in the UK and internationally, generating research collaborations, producing MDE training material, and increasing awareness of MDE in the software industry. mde-network.com

item.title: Service
item.description:
2026

  • ACM/IEEE International Conference on Model Driven Engineering Languages and Systems (MODELS 2026) — PC member.
  • 4th International Workshop on Agile Model-Driven Engineering (Agile MDE 2026) — PC member. Part of STAF 2026 · Rennes, France
  • 22nd European Conference on Modelling Foundations and Applications (ECMFA 2026) — PC member. Part of STAF 2026 · Rennes, France
  • iFS Steering Committee — Steering committee member. Joint steering committee for the integrated Formal methods and Software Engineering community, elected by the FASE Steering Committee (vote February 2026).
  • 29th International Conference on Fundamental Approaches to Software Engineering (FASE 2026) — PC member. Part of European joint conferences on theory and practice of software (ETAPS 2026) · Turin, Italy · April 2026

2025

  • MoDELS - New Ideas and Emerging Results (NIER) — Programme Committee. Model Driven Engineering Languages and Systems · Grand Rapids, MI, USA · 5-10 October 2025
  • MoDELS (Artifact Evaluation Committee) — Programme Committee. Model Driven Engineering Languages and Systems · Grand Rapids, MI, USA · 5-10 October 2025
  • International Journal on Software and Systems Modeling (SoSyM) — Reviewer.
  • 3rd Workshop on Agile MDE — PC member. Part of STAF 2025 · Koblenz, Germany
  • 28th International Conference on Fundamental Approaches to Software Engineering (FASE 2025) — Co-chair. Part of European joint conferences on theory and practice of software (ETAPS 2025) · Hamilton, Canada · May 2025
  • Irish Research Council - postgraduate/postdoctoral programmes 2024/2025 — Remote reviewer.

2024

  • 20th European Conference on Modelling Foundations and Applications (ECMFA 2024) — Programme Committee. Part of Software Technologies: Applications and Foundations (STAF 2024) · Enschede, Netherlands · July 2024
  • MoDELS (Artifact Evaluation Committee) — Programme Committee. Model Driven Engineering Languages and Systems · Linz, Austria · 22-27 September 2024
  • 2nd Workshop on Agile MDE — PC member. Part of STAF 2024 · Enschede, Netherlands
  • Irish Research Council - postgraduate/postdoctoral programmes 2023/2024 — Remote reviewer.

2023

  • 15th Transformation Tool Contest (TTC 2023) — Co-chair. Part of the Software Technologies: Applications and Foundations (STAF) federated conferences. · Leicester, UK · 21-07-2023
  • MoDELS (Tools & Demonstrations) — Programme Committee. Model Driven Engineering Languages and Systems · Västerås, Sweden · 1-6 October 2023
  • Software Technologies - Applications and Foundations (STAF 2023) — Workshop chair. Software Technologies: Applications and Foundations (STAF) workshops. · Leicester, UK · 17-07-2023 to 21-07-2023
  • Journal of Object Technology — Reviewer.
  • International Journal on Software and Systems Modeling (SoSyM) — Reviewer.
  • Journal of Logical and Algebraic Methods in Programming — Reviewer. Elsevier
  • Journal of Knowledge and Information Systems — Reviewer. Springer
  • Journal of Science of Computing Programming — Reviewer. Elsevier
  • Irish Research Council - postgraduate/postdoctoral programmes 2023/2024 — Remote reviewer.

2022

  • Journal of Object Technology — Reviewer.
  • MDENet Annual symposium — Co-author in talk. MDENet: the EPSRC expert network on MDE · King’s College, UK · 02-12-2022
  • MoDELS (Tools & Demonstrations) — Programme Committee. Model Driven Engineering Languages and Systems · Montréal, Canada · 23-28 October
  • MDENet thematic workshop on Systems Engineering — Participant. MDENet: the EPSRC expert network on MDE · King’s College, UK · 11-05-2022
  • MDENet student showcase competition — Panel Member. MDENet: the EPSRC expert network on MDE
  • Journal of Logical and Algebraic Methods in Programming — Reviewer. Elsevier
  • International Journal on Empirical Software Engineering — Reviewer. Springer
  • Agencia Estatal de Investigación, Ministerio de Ciencia e Innovación (Spain) — External reviewer. Red.es. Proyectos investigación IA y otras tecnologías digitales y su integración 2021
  • Irish Research Council - postgraduate programme 2021/2022 — Remote reviewer.

2021

  • MDENet thematic workshop on AI / data science — Participant. MDENet: the EPSRC expert network on MDE · Online · 25-11-2021
  • 14th Transformation Tool Contest (TTC 2021) — Co-chair. Part of the Software Technologies: Applications and Foundations (STAF) federated conferences. · Bergen, Norway · 25-06-2021
  • 14th Innovations in Software Engineering Conference (ISEC) — Programme committee. KIIT, University, Bhubaneswar · 25-02-2021 to 27-02-2021
  • Fundamental Approaches to Software Engineering (FASE 2021) — Programme committee. Part of ETAPS 2021 · Luxembourg-Ville, Luxembourg · 27-03-2021 to 01-04-2021
  • Irish Research Council - postgraduate programme 2020/2021 — Remote reviewer.
  • Irish Research Council - postdoctoral programme 2020/2021 — Remote reviewer.

2020

  • (SLE 2020) — Programme committee. Co-located with SPLASH 2020 · Chicago, USA · 15-11-2020 to 20-11-2020
  • 14th Transformation Tool Contest (TTC 2020) — Co-chair. Part of the Software Technologies: Applications and Foundations (STAF) federated conferences. · Bergen, Norway · 26-06-2020
  • 13th International Workshop on Rewriting Logic and its Applications (WRLA 2020) — Programme committee. Part of ETAPS 2020 · Dublin, Ireland · 25-04-2020 to 26-04-2020
  • International Journal on Software and Systems Modeling (SoSyM) — Reviewer.
  • Journal Science of Computer Programming. Elsevier. — Reviewer.
  • Irish Research Council - postdoctoral programme 2019/2020 — Remote reviewer.

  • International Journal on Software and Systems Modeling (SoSyM) — Reviewer.
  • Journal Science of Computer Programming. Elsevier. — Reviewer.
  • Irish Research Council - postgraduate programme 2019/2020 — Remote reviewer.

2005

item.title: Talks
item.description:
Invited Talks

  • Composing Clinical Activity Guidance for Multimorbidity via Bounded Relational Analysis (Mar 2026) LLHRG Showcasing and Networking Event, Leicester Tigers Rugby Club, Leicester, UK.

  • P-STEP: Personalised Space Technology Exercise Platform (2025) MedTech @ Leicester 2025, together with James Donaldson (Department of Cardiovascular Sciences), University of Leicester, Leicester, UK.

  • Models in Motion: Agile MDE for Continuous Adaptation (Jun 2025) Agile MDE 2025 (workshop at STAF 2025), Koblenz, Germany. [Programme]

  • Software Reuse in Modeling Language Engineering using Structural and Behavioural Model Subtyping with OCL Constraints (Feb 2022) GReTA — Graph Transformation Theory and Applications, Online (recording available). [Event]

  • EMF-Syncer: helping the ‘impatient’ programmer (or…adapting to reality) (Mar 2022) MDENet Research Demonstration, Online. [Recording]

  • Managing model transformations at Scale with YAMTL (Apr 2021) Scalable Management of Low-Code Artefacts Workshop (Lowcomote), Online. [Recording]

  • YAMTL — transforming models from within your own code (Oct 2021) MDENet Research Demonstration, Online. [Recording]

item.title: Teaching
item.description:
Administrative Roles

BSc Director, Computer Science and Software Engineering programmes, School of Computing and Mathematical Sciences, University of Leicester — 2018 - 2023

Coordination of modules in undergraduate programmes ‘Computer Science’, ‘Software Engineering’ and BSc part (three first years) of ‘MComp’.

Chair of the Industrial Advisory Board of the School of Computing and Mathematical Sciences, University of Leicester — since 2023

Modules
